nativeRange.habitat: Rocky overhangs, caves, and crevices in reef-associated environments.

diet.notes: Generally accepts frozen and prepared carnivore foods including mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, and other meaty marine foods. Recommended to feed once daily in reef aquaria.

summary: A captive-bred hybrid of the Orchid and Striped Dottybacks, the Electric Indigo Dottyback displays a striking tricolor pattern and tends to be rather peaceful compared to other dottybacks, making it generally suitable for reef tanks.
careNotes: The Electric Indigo Dottyback is a captive-bred hybrid cross between Pseudochromis fridmani (the Orchid Dottyback, native to the Red Sea) and Pseudochromis sankeyi (the Striped or Schooling Dottyback, native to the southern Red Sea and Gulf of Aden). It does not occur in the wild. The hybrid displays a striking tricolor pattern — turquoise-blue on top, a defined black band running along the midline, and white on the lower portion — along with an elegant caudal filament inherited from its P. sankeyi parentage. Like its parent species, it has been reported to shift color depending on mood or stress level. While generally considered more peaceful than most dottybacks, it tends to be semi-aggressive toward conspecifics and other dottyback species, and is generally advised to be kept singly unless the aquarium is particularly large. It has been described as posing minimal risk to ornamental shrimp, though caution with very small shrimp may be warranted. Ample live rock, with caves, overhangs, and crevices to shelter in and claim as territory, is strongly recommended. Note that this hybrid has been listed as not currently in production, so availability may be limited.
breedingNotes: The Electric Indigo Dottyback is a first-generation captive hybrid and has not been documented breeding in home aquaria. Its parent species, Pseudochromis sankeyi, has been successfully reared in captivity.

additional.name: orafarm.com
additional.notes: Secondary source. Confirmed hybrid parentage (P. fridmani x P. sankeyi), tricolor description, and 'rather peaceful' temperament; no numeric parameters provided. Fish listed as Not In Production.

additional.name: FishBase (Pseudochromis fridmani)
additional.notes: Used for cross-verification of adult size (max 6.3 cm), temperature range (23.7–29.3°C), native range (Red Sea), taxonomy (Pseudochromidae / Ovalentaria), and habitat (vertical rock faces, overhangs, reef-associated).

additional.name: FishBase (Pseudochromis sankeyi)
additional.notes: Cross-verification of parent species data: max 7.0 cm, temperature 24–27°C, native range (southern Red Sea, Gulf of Aden, Socotra). Reef2Reef wiki returned 403; BlueZoo and Fishkeeping World returned 404 for this species.

fish.breedingNotes: A captive-bred hybrid; breeding in home aquaria has not been documented.
